lifecycle.note: First end-to-end render of the kit.

blocks.title: One JSON file in, a beautiful agent-readable page out
blocks.lede: Dossier renders a single document model into a self-contained, themed, interactive HTML artifact. The page you see is a projection of the embedded **[[dossier-model]]** data island, so agents read one block while humans get the rendered view.

sideCards.label: Status
sideCards.note: First full render of the catalog.

sideCards.label: Render model
sideCards.note: Static HTML + JSON island + minimal JS.

cards.title: For humans
cards.body: A polished, themed reading surface with TOC, search, command palette, and one-click export.

cards.title: For agents
cards.body: The full structured model lives in `#dossier-model`, read one block, no DOM scraping.

cards.title: For your wiki
cards.body: Self-contained files embed via iframe and link with `[[slug]]` cross-references.

blocks.title: How a Brief is produced
blocks.subtitle: The agent writes JSON; the generator owns all design and interactivity.

steps.title: Author
steps.body: An agent emits a `brief.json` conforming to the schema.

steps.title: Validate
steps.body: The generator lints the model and assigns stable block ids.

steps.title: Render
steps.body: JSON becomes HTML + the embedded data island + Markdown.

steps.title: Use
steps.body: Read, review, export, embed, or round-trip back to an agent.

left.title: Composable.
left.body: Sections, two-col, and tabs nest other blocks, so layouts build up from the same primitives.

right.title: Self-contained.
right.body: No external network calls; everything inlines so the file works offline and emails cleanly.

phases.label: Phase 2
phases.body: Generator engine + data island + Markdown export.

blocks.code:
node bin/dossier.mjs build examples/sample.dossier.json
# -> writes dossier-overview.html + .md

items.q: Does it replace my docs site?
items.a: No. It is a companion: linkable, embeddable, exportable artifacts alongside your normal wiki.
They parse the `#dossier-model` JSON island, or consume the Markdown / agent-digest exports.

risks.mitigation: Build catalog first, then features against a frozen schema.

items.label: Deep-dive reference
items.use: Baseline for the read-only Brief layout.

items.title: Dogfood MCP process packets in another agent.

items.statement: Briefs usually sit in the same folder, so relative [[slug]] links suffice.
Should the catalog generator (#26) come back later as an index Brief?

terms.definition: The embedded JSON data island that is the source of truth for the rendered page.
A compact, token-efficient text summary of the Brief for downstream agents.

blocks.title: Pipeline
blocks.spec: digraph { rankdir=LR; bgcolor="transparent"; node [shape=box style=rounded fontname="Inter" fontsize=12 color="#c81e4a" fontcolor="#1a1822"]; edge [color="#8b8698"]; "brief.json" -> "generator"; "generator" -> "brief.html"; "generator" -> "brief.md"; "brief.html" -> "#dossier-model island"; }

candidates.title: Command palette
candidates.summary: Cmd-K to jump to sections and run document actions.
candidates.body:
Opens with **Cmd/Ctrl-K**. Lists every section heading plus document actions (toggle theme, copy Markdown, download JSON, view source). Arrow keys move, Enter activates, Esc closes.

The command list is built at render time from the table of contents, so it stays in sync with the document automatically.

blocks.code:
// commands are derived from the embedded model
const commands = toc.map(t => ({ label: t.label, run: () => jump(t.id) }))

candidates.title: Changelog / version diff
candidates.summary: Diff the embedded model against a prior version's JSON.
candidates.body: Because every Brief embeds its full source model, two versions can be compared structurally rather than as text. The diff highlights added, removed, and changed blocks and surfaces a per-version changelog.

candidates.title: Syntax highlighting (Shiki)
candidates.summary: Build-time, theme-aware highlighting with zero client JS.
candidates.body: Run Shiki at generate time to emit pre-highlighted, inline-styled code with light and dark variants driven by CSS variables. No client runtime and no network: the highlighted HTML is baked into the file.

candidates.title: Diagram to inline SVG
candidates.summary: Render Mermaid or D2 to static SVG at build time.
candidates.body: Use a browserless WASM renderer (Graphviz or D2) to turn diagram specs into inline SVG during generation, keeping the artifact self-contained with no headless browser dependency.
